mechanism_narrative: Monthly acceleration in industrial production predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns because stronger output signals higher corporate earnings. This relationship is always active.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO monthly change and 20-day SPX returns is below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, restrictive monetary policy raises discount rates and debt-service costs enough to produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rising unemployment and falling industrial production identify a recessionary impulse that forces equity risk premiums higher, generating negative returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases over prior 3 months and INDPRO decreases over prior 3 months
failure_condition: Joint z-score combination of UNRATE and INDPRO fails to produce a Rank IC greater than 0.04 against 60-day forward returns over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth predicts positive equity returns during low-inflation regimes because demand-driven revenue expansion lifts earnings expectations without triggering aggressive Fed tightening.
failure_condition: RSAFS monthly changes show no positive correlation with 20-day SPX returns (Rank IC below 0.03) during periods when CPIAUCSL 12-month change is below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Changes in housing starts lead the business cycle and predict short-term equity returns through construction multiplier effects on employment and materials demand.
failure_condition: HOUST changes do not positively predict 5-day SPX returns with Rank IC above 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll gains exceeding 300 thousand signal an overheating labor market that increases the probability of aggressive monetary tightening, producing negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in fewer than 45% of months where PAYEMS increases by more than 300 thousand.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth surprises revise aggregate earnings expectations upward, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarterly changes fail to achieve Rank IC above 0.03 against 60-day SPX returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated inflation and high short-term interest rates jointly raises real discount rates and compresses profit margins, producing negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint signal of elevated CPIAUCSL and high FEDFUNDS does not produce negative 5-day SPX returns in at least 55% of triggered instances.
